# Memory Maintenance

- Shared memories are generated, reviewed, committed, and read-only.
- Local memories live under `local/`, are maintained automatically when tasks establish verified durable project knowledge, and are never committed.
- The model authors only `local/current-task`; `scripts/memory/**` validates candidate bundles and renders managed local index, durable, conflict, episode, archive, and state records.
- Raw episodes, archives, and machine state are hidden from Serena memory tools and must never contain transcripts, payloads, logs, credentials, or customer data.
- Shared memories summarize canonical repository authorities; they never override AGENTS or architecture documents.
- Navigate through `mem:core` and use `mem:` references so integrity checks can detect stale links.
- Regenerate with `pnpm serena:memories`; do not hand-edit generated files.
